---
title: "Assay Performance Testing"
output: html_document
date: "2026-01-26"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
# load libraries
library(tidyverse)
library(tidyr)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)
library(svglite)
library(base64enc)

# The aim of this R script is for internal evaluation of new primer designs. We call each design/target as assay, made up of one
# forward and one reverse primer. We then combine these assays in a 4-plex reaction. Do these primers work together as a 4-plex and
# maintain specificity? That is the question. To fail means an assay needs to be reworked or dropped from the multiplex. The output 
# from the experiment is a relative florescence intensity (RFI), such as the kind you might get from qPCR with limited cycles (not 
# to plateau but to the inflection point, thus different for different template staring amounts)

# What this script seeks to do it provide a summary report that a lab RA can read, check for pass or fail falgs. If all pass, they 
# can proceed with the assay applied to real samples. If something fails, they can escalate that to a senior design person/manager 
# and the report also will indicate which assay is failing, and in what respect (off-target, lower than expected abundance, etc.)

```

```{r define assay, include=TRUE}
# we assume each assay is on a different color channel, up to 4. Data from the reader is converted from RFI to a count of molecules 
# based on standard curves, or for ddPCR, direct counting after error correction.

# target name by color channel. Must include Ch number as prefix else graph will mis-order alphabetically. Here we append the primer target
# after the channel indicator, to simplify downstream evaluation of performance.
Ch01_target_name <- "Ch01_EGR1"
Ch02_target_name <- "Ch02_HTT"
Ch03_target_name <- "Ch03_ZNF123"
Ch04_target_name <- "Ch04_MED23"

# thresholds and flags for metrics we use to determine assay performance and multiplex capacity. These values are corrected molecule counts.

# no primers and no template, the technical background.
channel_background_signal <- 1000
# for each assay run alone, there is a minimum count we expect. else it means the singleplex design is BAD from the start
single_one_threshold <- 50
# no template blanks, should be zero ideally, either single or as a multiplex PCR reaction.
single_water_threshold <- 100
multi_water_threshold <- 200
# these are the thresholds for comparing counts between single and multiplex, the fraction off we still accept as passing
single_all_threshold <- 0.15
multi_all_threshold <- 0.25
multi_all_ave_threshold <- 0.10

```

```{r dataframe, include=TRUE}
options("scipen"=2, "digits"=4)

files_to_open <- list.files(pattern = ".*basic.*\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
if (length(files_to_open) > 0) {file_pointer_basic <- files_to_open[1]}

# for the basic testing of a 4-plex design
df_counts <- read.csv(file_pointer_basic)

```

```{r basic barchart, include=TRUE}
# Wrangle the df based on what columns we need and which are superfluous, based on what we know. A defined structure. The file was
# designed by a different team, so we can't change the organization of columns/data. Hence, we need to clean up the data here.

# remove unneeded rows
df_counts <- df_counts[-c(1, 2, 3), ]
# remove unneeded columns
df_counts <- df_counts[, 2:ncol(df_counts)]
# make the top row the column headers
names(df_counts) <- df_counts[1, ]
df_counts <- df_counts[-1, ]
# rename to make simpler
colnames(df_counts)[1] <- "condition"
colnames(df_counts)[2] <- Ch01_target_name
colnames(df_counts)[3] <- Ch02_target_name
colnames(df_counts)[4] <- Ch03_target_name
colnames(df_counts)[5] <- Ch04_target_name
# convert to numbers
df_counts[, Ch01_target_name] <- as.numeric(df_counts[, Ch01_target_name])
df_counts[, Ch02_target_name] <- as.numeric(df_counts[, Ch02_target_name])
df_counts[, Ch03_target_name] <- as.numeric(df_counts[, Ch03_target_name])
df_counts[, Ch04_target_name] <- as.numeric(df_counts[, Ch04_target_name])
# convert 0 to a small # for log scale graphing
df_counts <- df_counts %>% mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~ if_else(.x == 0, 1, .x)))
# label by set in new col, first 5 chars, so set_all will be set_a
df_counts <- df_counts %>% mutate(set_type = substr(df_counts$condition, 1, 5))

# ---

pivot_metrics <- df_counts %>% pivot_longer(cols = starts_with("Ch"), names_to = "channel", values_to = "counts")

data_plot <- pivot_metrics %>% ggplot(aes(x = c(condition), y = counts, fill=channel)) +
  geom_bar(position = "dodge", stat = "identity") + 
  theme_minimal() +
  geom_hline(yintercept=50, linetype="solid", color="black", linewidth=1.0, alpha=0.7) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("blue","green","orange","red")) + 

  ggtitle("Assay Testing 4-plex") + 
  labs(x = "condition", y = "counts in 50uL", color = "black") +
  theme(plot.title = element_text(hjust=0.5)) +
  theme(legend.title = element_blank()) + 
  theme(legend.position="right") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=0.5)) +
  theme(axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12,color = "black",face = "bold")) +
  theme(axis.title.y = element_text(size = 12,color = "black",face = "bold")) + 
  theme(title = element_text(size = 12,color = "black",face = "bold"))

ggsave(file="assay_basic.png", plot=data_plot, width=7, height=5)
# Read the png and encode it as raw vector. Convert to character string
encoded_string <- base64encode("assay_basic.png")

```

```{r channel loop and report, include=TRUE}
# This section takes the image of the assay performance and all of the threshold values, and converts them into an HTML file for easy 
# reading, sharing, and archiving. This is a hack way to do this in R, but it works. Just took a bit to code and is hard to modify as
# new fields need to be added, etc. 

#blank out a new file and add headers
cat("<h1>VALIDATION REPORT</h1>\n", file = "report_file.html", append = FALSE)
cat("<p>This report</p>\n", file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
cat("<hr />\n", file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
cat("<h3>Multiplexing Specificity</h3>\n", file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)

# the bar chart 
cat("<img src=\"data:image/png;base64,", file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
cat(encoded_string, file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
cat("\" alt=\"assay performance bar chart\" width=700 height=500>\n", file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)

# generate the summary for each channel/target in the multiplex. This way we can see which assays are problematic and which ones
# perform well as single target PCR or in multiplex.
channel_list <- list(Ch01_target_name, Ch02_target_name, Ch03_target_name, Ch04_target_name)

for (which_channel in channel_list) {
  channel_working <- TRUE
  single_one_pass <- TRUE
  single_water_pass <- TRUE
  single_all_pass <- TRUE
  multi_all_pass <- TRUE
  multi_all_ave_pass <- TRUE
  multi_water_pass <- TRUE
  # channel check on fatal errors - if it can't find the data column that matches this channel, abort the analysis and give error
  # find which primer set this channel_target belongs to - in the first 4 rows. Which has value > VALUE else too high background
  if (df_counts[1, which_channel] > channel_background_signal) {
    primer_set <- "set_1"
  } else if (df_counts[2, which_channel] > channel_background_signal) {
    primer_set <- "set_2"
  } else if (df_counts[3, which_channel] > channel_background_signal) {
    primer_set <- "set_3"
  } else if (df_counts[4, which_channel] > channel_background_signal) {
    primer_set <- "set_4"
  } else {
    channel_working <- FALSE
  }
  
  if(channel_working){
    # group by set, check each in turn for the passing criteria within each set
    check_set <- df_counts %>% filter(str_detect(set_type, primer_set) | str_detect(set_type, "set_a"))
    single_one_counts <- check_set[1, which_channel]
    single_water_counts <- check_set[2, which_channel] 
    single_all_counts <- check_set[3, which_channel] 
    multi_all_counts_1 <- check_set[4, which_channel] 
    multi_all_counts_2 <- check_set[5, which_channel] 
    multi_all_average <- (multi_all_counts_1 + multi_all_counts_2)/2
    multi_water_counts_1 <- check_set[6, which_channel] 
    multi_water_counts_2 <- check_set[7, which_channel] 
    multi_water_average <- (multi_water_counts_1 + multi_water_counts_2)/2
    
    # --- threshold checks
    
    # off target (non template channels) in 1P1T is high, > VALUE, filter to pick out row one and only the non-target counts
    background_counts <- check_set %>% filter(!str_detect(set_type, primer_set) | str_detect(set_type, "set_a"))
    background_counts <- check_set %>% select(-1,-6) %>% select(-all_of(which_channel))
    background_counts <- background_counts[1,]
    # there will be 3 columns, check each to see if any are over threshold
    if((background_counts[,1] > single_one_threshold) | (background_counts[,2] > single_one_threshold) | (background_counts[,3] > single_one_threshold)){
      single_one_pass <- FALSE
      }
    # water blank single is high
    if(single_water_counts > single_water_threshold){single_water_pass <- FALSE}
    # counts deviate more than X% when singleplex with all template, relative to singleplex to one template
    fraction_deviation <- (single_all_threshold * single_all_counts) - abs(single_all_counts - single_one_counts)
    if(fraction_deviation < 0){single_all_pass <- FALSE}
    # counts deviate more than X% when multiplex with all template, relative to singleplex to one template
    fraction_deviation <- (multi_all_threshold * multi_all_average) - abs(multi_all_average - single_one_counts)
    if(fraction_deviation < 0){multi_all_pass <- FALSE}
    # duplicate counts compared for x% deviation, in the multiplex with all templates
    fraction_deviation <- (multi_all_ave_threshold * multi_all_counts_1) - abs(multi_all_counts_1 - multi_all_counts_2)
    if(fraction_deviation < 0){multi_all_ave_pass <- FALSE}
    # water blank multiplex is high, > VALUE
    if(multi_water_average > multi_water_threshold){multi_water_pass <- FALSE}
  }
    
  status_text <- ""
  verbose_text <- ""
  
  # to debug
  #channel_working <- FALSE
  #single_one_pass <- FALSE
  #single_water_pass <- FALSE
  #single_all_pass <- FALSE
  #multi_all_pass <- FALSE
  #multi_all_ave_pass <- FALSE
  #multi_water_pass <- FALSE
  
  cat("<h3>Summary of assay performance for ", which_channel, "</h3>\n\n", file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
  
  row_1_string <- "<table style='border-collapse: collapse; width: 70%;' border=1><tbody><tr><td style='width: 20%;text-align: center;'><strong>Assay test type</td><td style='width: 10%;text-align: center;'><strong>Count limit</td><td style='width: 10%;text-align: center;'><strong>Fraction limit</td><td style='width: 10%;text-align: center;'><strong>Status</td><td style='width: 50%;text-align: center;'><strong>Warning message</td></tr>"
  cat(row_1_string, file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
  
  if(channel_working){
    status_text = "PASS"; verbose_text = ""
    row_2_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Background signal</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(channel_background_signal),"</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr>")
  } else {
    status_text = "ERROR"; verbose_text = "Background signal is too high"
    row_2_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Background signal</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(channel_background_signal),"</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;background-color: red;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr></table>")
  }
  cat(row_2_string, file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
  
  # else end or table - no more analysis to output
  if(channel_working){
    if(single_one_pass){status_text = "PASS"; verbose_text = ""
      row_3_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Single assay to single template</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(single_one_threshold),"</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr>")
    } else {
      status_text = "WARNING"; verbose_text = "Single assay against its single template is not specific"
      row_3_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Single assay to single template</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(single_one_threshold),"</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;background-color: yellow;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr>")
    }
    cat(row_3_string, file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
    
    if(single_water_pass){status_text = "PASS"; verbose_text = ""
      row_4_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Single assay in water</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(single_water_threshold),"</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr>")
    } else {
      status_text = "WARNING"; verbose_text = "Single assay in water has high counts"
      row_4_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Single assay in water</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(single_water_threshold),"</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;background-color: yellow;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr>")
    }  
    cat(row_4_string, file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
    
    if(single_all_pass){status_text = "PASS"; verbose_text = ""
      row_5_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Single assay to all templates</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(single_all_threshold),"</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr>")
    } else {
      status_text = "WARNING"; verbose_text = "Single assay against all templates is not specific"
      row_5_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Single assay to all templates</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(single_all_threshold),"</td><td style='text-align:center;background-color: yellow;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr>")
    }
    cat(row_5_string, file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
    
    if(multi_all_pass){status_text = "PASS"; verbose_text = ""
      row_6_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Multiplex against all templates</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(multi_all_threshold),"</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr>")
    } else {
      status_text = "WARNING"; verbose_text = "Multiplex against all templates is not specific"
      row_6_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Multiplex against all templates</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(multi_all_threshold),"</td><td style='text-align:center;background-color: yellow;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr>")
    }
    cat(row_6_string, file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
    
    if(multi_all_ave_pass){status_text = "PASS"; verbose_text = ""
      row_7_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Multiplex replicates</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(multi_all_ave_threshold),"</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr>")
    } else {
      status_text = "WARNING"; verbose_text = "Multiplex replicates deviate above threshold"
      row_7_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Multiplex replicates</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(multi_all_ave_threshold),"</td><td style='text-align:center;background-color: yellow;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr>")
    }
    cat(row_7_string, file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
    
    if(multi_water_pass){status_text = "PASS"; verbose_text = ""
      row_8_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Multiplex in water</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(multi_water_threshold),"</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr></tbody></table>\n")
    } else {
      status_text = "WARNING"; verbose_text = "Multiplex assay in water has high counts"
      row_8_string <- paste0("<tr><td>Multiplex in water</td><td style='text-align:center;'>",as.character(multi_water_threshold),"</td><td style='text-align:center;'>-</td><td style='text-align:center;background-color: yellow;'>",status_text,"</td><td>",verbose_text,"</td></tr></tbody></table>\n")
    }
    cat(row_8_string, file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)
  }
  cat("<hr />\n", file = "report_file.html", append = TRUE)

}

```
